task_completion.description: The document is saved as inspection_report.docx
task_completion.visual_evidence: The title bar of the word processor (e.g., LibreOffice Writer) displays 'inspection_report.docx'.

task_completion.description: Primary sections use Heading 1, subsections use Heading 2, and a multi-level numbered outline is applied.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Section titles like 'Introduction', 'Structural Observations', etc., appear in a large, bold Heading 1 style with hierarchical numbering (e.g., '1. Introduction', '1.1 Subsection').

task_completion.description: The seven structural deficiency observations are formatted into 3-column tables with bold headers, visible borders, and bold labels.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Under 'Structural Observations', there are multiple 3-column tables with visible grid borders. The headers 'Location', 'Deficiency Description', and 'Recommended Action' are bolded. Above each table is a bolded label like 'OBS-001 (Critical):'.

task_completion.description: The three load calculations are formatted into 4-column tables with bold headers and visible borders.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Under 'Structural Calculations', there are 4-column tables with visible grid borders. The headers 'Parameter', 'Value', 'Unit', and 'Code Reference' are bolded.

task_completion.description: Figure captions are applied to the five figures.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Images/figures in the document have distinct caption text attached to them (e.g., 'Figure 1: ...'), typically formatted in a smaller or italicized font standard for captions.

task_completion.description: The Professional Engineer Certification section contains a bordered single-cell table with the required certification text and license number.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Under 'Professional Engineer Certification', there is a single-cell table (a box with visible borders) containing the text 'I hereby certify...', the license number 'TX-78234', and other certification details.

integrity.description: The agent used a GUI word processor (like LibreOffice Writer) to format the document.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the LibreOffice Writer interface open with the document being edited, rather than just terminal windows running automated scripts.

integrity.description: The agent created actual word processor tables rather than simulating them with text alignment.
integrity.visual_evidence: Tables have actual structural borders and cell boundaries typical of word processor tables, not just text separated by spaces, tabs, or ASCII characters (like '|' or '-').

integrity.description: The agent did not use a python script or pandoc command in the terminal to bypass manual GUI formatting.
integrity.visual_evidence: The terminal history does not show commands like `python format_doc.py` or `pandoc` being used to generate the final docx file directly.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The output file must be named 'inspection_report.docx'. There are exactly 7 primary section headings: Introduction, Scope of Assessment, Building Description, Structural Observations, Structural Calculations, Conclusions and Recommendations, Professional Engineer Certification. There are exactly 7 structural observations (OBS-001 to OBS-007) and 3 structural calculations. The PE license number is TX-78234 and the certification text includes 'I hereby certify that this structural assessment was prepared by me or under my direct supervision'.
